comment: R36 — Allowlist for legitimate dynamic imports. Each entry must carry a reason. The checker downgrades these from warning to info.

allow.reason: CLI subcommand lazy-load — context build/refresh/status modules are only used when those subcommands fire.
CLI subcommand lazy-load — ci predict and would-fail modules only loaded when their subcommand fires.
CLI subcommand lazy-load — registry-lifecycle is a rarely-used sibling check.
Contract gate path is only used when --contract is passed.
Watcher + HTTP transport are optional MCP runtime features that should not be eagerly loaded for the stdio default path.
Language profile detection is heavy and only needed for the export subcommand.
Language profile detection is heavy and only needed for repository map builders.
Feature-bundle reader is only loaded for bundle-aware policy evaluation paths.
Contract hash computation only fires when --include-contract is passed; keeps the default handoff path light.
Heavy doctors (self-config doctor + pack contributions inventory) only run when the user requested the corresponding fix kind.
Search-tuning + test-runner loaders only fire for the corresponding doctor check passes; lazy avoids paying their cost in the unrelated paths.
node:fs lazy-loaded inside an async glob resolver to keep the module's synchronous load path side-effect-free.
Plugin lifecycle profile registry only matters when plan-simulation runs against a plugin lifecycle plan.
Ingest body extractor is heavy and only loaded inside the ingest-apply path.
